response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist software craftsman with 15+ years of experience, strong engineering fundamentals, and some high-throughput distributed systems exposure, but the resume shows no direct experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing, feature flagging, growth engineering, or metrics/telemetry infrastructure — the core of this Principal-level role. Career history is largely senior/staff IC work at small-to-mid-size companies rather than the multi-workstream, cross-org platform leadership expected at IC6.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, backend, embedded, and mobile with strong testing/TDD craftsmanship
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling (MCP code generators), matching the AI-first emphasis
concerns: No demonstrated experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience
No evidence of Principal-scope leadership: multi-team initiatives, org-wide architecture ownership, or mentoring at scale
Limited depth in statistical analysis, metrics design, and telemetry foundations required by the role

key_factors.factor: Scope gap between senior/staff IC roles and Principal (IC6) expectations
Strong general engineering fundamentals and distributed systems background
